# Striatum: MCP Claim Loop

This is the core of driving a Striatum run. You will repeat this
loop until the run hits a terminal state.

## When to invoke

- The operator asked to claim work / drive a run that's already
  `ready` or `running`.
- A work packet was returned by `work.await_packet` and you need to act
  on it.
- A required artifact needs to be published.

## Authoritative reference

- `striatum register-session` — Open an agent session for a (role, lane) pair.
- `striatum claim-next` — Claim the next ready work packet for a session.
- `striatum ack` — Acknowledge a claimed packet and start work.
- `striatum heartbeat` — Extend the active lease while work continues.
- `striatum publish-artifact` — Publish a build artifact for a job.
- `striatum verdict` — Record a non-finding verdict for a review job.
- `striatum submit-review` — Publish a finding artifact + verdict in one call.
- `striatum complete` — Mark a non-review job complete.
- `striatum worktree create` — Create the per-job git worktree (RFC 0008).
- `striatum worktree release` — Release a per-job worktree when work is done.

Call daemon MCP `tools/list` first, then use `tools/call` for the daemon
methods below. `striatum <verb> --help` documents the CLI compatibility
fallback for the same daemon methods.

## Common patterns

```json
{"method":"tools/list","params":{"repository_id":"<repository-id>"}}
{"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"session.register","repository_id":"<repository-id>","arguments":{"run_id":"<run-id>","role":"<role>","lane":"<lane>"}}}
{"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"work.await_packet","repository_id":"<repository-id>","arguments":{"session_id":"<session-id>","lease_seconds":1800}}}
{"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"work.ack","repository_id":"<repository-id>","arguments":{"session_id":"<session-id>","message_id":"<message-id>","lease_id":"<lease-id>"}}}
{"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"artifact.publish","repository_id":"<repository-id>","arguments":{"session_id":"<session-id>","job_id":"<job-id>","lease_id":"<lease-id>","kind":"<kind>","logical_name":"<logical-name>","path":"<repo-relative-path>"}}}
{"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"work.complete","repository_id":"<repository-id>","arguments":{"session_id":"<session-id>","job_id":"<job-id>","lease_id":"<lease-id>","summary":"completed"}}}
```

For review jobs, call `review.submit` with `verdict` set to one of
`accept`, `accept_with_findings`, `needs_revision`, or `reject`. If a packet
has `worktree_required: true`, call `worktree.create` before
`artifact.publish` and `worktree.release` after completion. The packet's
`commands` block supplies exact CLI fallback invocations and the ids you need
for these MCP arguments.
```

## Front-matter kinds (V1)

When publishing Markdown artifacts whose kind has a registered
schema, include the front matter the publisher expects. The
allowed kinds in this runner version are:

- action_item_ledger
- auto_finalize_gate_evidence
- commit_request
- decision
- escalation
- finding
- findings_ledger
- handoff
- harness_improvement_proposal
- marker
- operator_brief
- operator_report
- other
- patch_summary
- pr_request
- progress_note
- prompt
- support_ledger
- synthesis
- test_report
- work_plan

The publisher refuses invalid front matter with exit code 6.

## Where to look next

- **`striatum-supervise`** — when the lane is held alive across
  packets.
- **`striatum-recover`** — when something looks stuck.

## What not to do

- Do not bypass the daemon (the CLI is the only client; the daemon is the single writer per D094 / RFC 0043); never open Postgres directly, and do not treat `.striatum/` scratch files as workflow state.
- Do not treat marker files, tmux panes, or terminal output as workflow state.
- Do not advance state by printing phrases; advance by calling the CLI verbs in your work packet.
- Do not capture stdout/stderr transcripts; D028 keeps them off by default.
- Do not derive bylines from job titles; use the byline supplied in your work packet.
- Do not parse a supervisor's own output for workflow state; supervisors send DEVNULL.
